message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1). Venv: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
/tmp/pass1_reconfirm.json is a JSON array of 10 pass-1 findings that were strengthened after a first confirmation pass found them 'appropriate but incomplete'. Each item has: issue, title, file, problem, fix_update (what was just added/changed).
Constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign, no metered API, engine imports no adapter, lazy optional backends.

You are independent CONFIRMER #2 of 2 for INDEX 7 (0-based) in /tmp/pass1_reconfirm.json.
Read that item (issue, problem, fix_update). Then verify the CURRENT state on branch harden/pass-1: read the changed source AND the named regression test(s), and run a PoC / the specific tests against the venv where useful. Decide: appropriate = the fix soundly addresses the root cause (no hack/over-claim); complete = it fully resolves the finding AND has a regression test that would actually catch a regression (a test that passes equally with/without the fix does NOT count). If the item documents a deliberately-deferred scope (e.g. #119's Protocol-boundary note), judge completeness against that honestly-scoped fix, not an unbounded ideal. Set either false with specifics if not met. Echo the issue number.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll start by reading the finding at index 7 in the JSON file.

content.content: 1	[{"issue": "102", "title": "run_json applies retries multiplicatively, causing a retry-storm and unbounded blocking time with no overall deadline", "file": "src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py", "sev": "HIGH", "problem": "`run_json` loops `MAX_RETRIES+1` times (line 531) and each iteration calls `_run_with_retries` (line 542), which itself retries up to `MAX_RETRIES` transient attempts AND up to `RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES` rate-limit attempts. The budgets multiply. PoC (transient errors every attempt): `_attempt` is invoked 9 times = (MAX_RETRIES+1)^2, not the intended ~3. Worse, the rate-limit path: per outer iteration the rate budget sleeps sum(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF*(r+1) for r in 0..4) = 450s; times 3 outer iteratio", "fix_update": "run_json now re-rolls ONLY on JSON parse failure (transient/rate/permanent propagate, no budget multiplication) + _sleep_bounded bounds inner backoff by the overall deadline. New tests: no-multiplication, seed re-roll on parse failure, deadline bound."}, {"issue": "111", "title": "Graph-orchestration gate decisions never enter the hash-chained provenance trail \u2014 the documented \u201cappend-only trail\u201d for the durable path is unchained, untamper-evident LangGraph state", "file": "/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py", "sev": "MEDIUM", "problem": "There are two governance mechanisms (ADR-0010): the synchronous run_study path records every gate decision into the hash-chained ProvenanceTrail (provenance.py), and the durable LangGraph interrupt/resume path (gatenode.make_gate_node) records decisions as plain dicts appended to a LangGraph state list 'gate_decisions'. GOVERNANCE \u00a73 states the chained ProvenanceTrail \"is the spine\u2026 No step can be retroactively removed or reordered,\" and GOVERNANCE.md lines 47-50 call the graph's gate_decisions ", "fix_update": "make_gate_node already accepts recorder= (bridges to ProvenanceTrail); now GOVERNANCE \u00a73 + graph.py docstring honestly scope that the durable graph path needs the recorder to be hash-chained. Test test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail."}, {"issue": "113", "title": "GateReview is a frozen dataclass whose Mapping payload is not frozen \u2014 mutable-in-place and unhashable (issue #7 escape)", "file": "src/assay_engine/pipeline.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "GateReview is declared @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True) with a `payload: Mapping[str, Any]` field but, unlike EVERY other frozen mapping-bearing value record in the engine (ProvenanceEntry, GateDecision, Corpus, ClaimRecord, Verdict, Hypothesis, BaselineArtifact, ReasoningRequest, etc.), it has no __post_init__ that normalizes the field through freeze_mapping. This reproduces both halves of the codebase's own documented issue #7 (see src/assay_engine/_frozen.py module docstring):\n\n1. Mutable-", "fix_update": "GateReview.__post_init__ freezes payload; NEW test test_gate_review_payload_is_frozen_and_hashable asserts FrozenDict + mutation raises + hashable."}, {"issue": "114", "title": "ProvenanceTrail.record performs a non-atomic check-then-act on shared list state (not thread-safe)", "file": "src/assay_engine/provenance.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "record() computes `seq = len(self._entries)` and `prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash` (read), then does work (clock, digest, freeze), then `self._entries.append(entry)` (write), with no lock. If a single ProvenanceTrail were shared across threads, two concurrent record() calls could read the same seq/prev_hash and append two entries that both claim e.g. seq=N with the same prev_hash, breaking the chain so verify_records() (which asserts e.seq == i and e.prev_hash == prev) would fail \u2014 a co", "fix_update": "record() lock-guarded; NEW test test_record_is_thread_safe_under_concurrency (8 threads x50, verify + contiguous seqs)."}, {"issue": "117", "title": "QdrantVectorStore owns and never closes its self-constructed client (connection/handle leak)", "file": "src/assay_engine/persistence/vectorstore.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "When no client is injected, `QdrantVectorStore.__init__` constructs its own `QdrantClient` via `get_qdrant_client()` (line 64) and stores it on `self._client`, but the class exposes no `close()`/context-manager and never closes the client. `QdrantClient` holds an underlying HTTP connection pool (httpx) / gRPC channel; for a self-constructed client the store is the sole owner, so the connection resources are leaked for the lifetime of every store instance with no deterministic release. Long-runni", "fix_update": "_owns_client + close()/context-manager; NEW test test_vector_store_closes_owned_client (owned client closed via get_qdrant_client + context exit)."}, {"issue": "118", "title": "QdrantVectorStore.upsert materializes all PointStructs (with per-vector copies) before sending any batch, so batching bounds payload but not memory", "file": "src/assay_engine/persistence/vectorstore.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "upsert builds the complete `points` list \u2014 one PointStruct per id, each holding `list(v)` (a fresh copy of the whole vector) \u2014 for the entire input before the chunking loop at line 88 sends anything. The comment claims chunking handles 'a large ingest', but it only bounds each HTTP request payload; peak process memory still holds the full corpus of vectors duplicated into PointStructs at once. For a real ingest (e.g. 5M docs x 768 float dims) this is the entire embedding set re-copied into Pytho", "fix_update": "upsert builds one batch at a time; NEW test test_vector_store_upsert_builds_one_batch_at_a_time asserts only batch_size vectors consumed per send (2,4,6 not 6,6,6)."}, {"issue": "119", "title": "corpus_fingerprint is recomputed redundantly per study (ingest + every build_baseline_artifact call), each a full canonical copy + sort + JSON of the whole corpus", "file": "src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "corpus_fingerprint builds a full list-comprehension copy of every unit and relation, sorts all units by id, canonicalizes every attribute mapping via _plain, and json.dumps the whole thing \u2014 O(total corpus bytes) time and memory. run_study computes it once at ingest (pipeline.py:280, `cfp`). Then build_baseline_artifact (determinism.py:104) computes it again from scratch for the determinism record's `input_hashes['corpus']` and `corpus_fingerprint`; a study's BaselineBuilder that calls build_bas", "fix_update": "build_baseline_artifact accepts corpus_hash (no recompute); NEW test test_build_baseline_reuses_precomputed_corpus_hash. NOTE: full e2e dedup across the BaselineBuilder Protocol boundary is deliberately deferred (would require a breaking build() signature change for a LOW perf item) \u2014 documented scope."}, {"issue": "120", "title": "Package version is hardcoded in two independent places (pyproject `version` and `__init__.__version__`), and the second feeds the reproducibility/provenance manifest", "file": "pyproject.toml", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "`pyproject.toml` sets `version = \"0.0.1\"` (line 7) while `src/assay_engine/__init__.py` separately hardcodes `__version__ = \"0.0.1\"` (line 28). These are two independent strings with no single source of truth \u2014 hatchling is not configured for dynamic versioning (`grep dynamic`/`hatch.version` returns nothing). This is not merely a style nit: `src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py` imports `__version__ as _ENGINE_VERSION` (line 24) and stamps it into the determinism/provenance record as `compo", "fix_update": "version dynamic + hatch.version path; NEW tests in test_packaging.py (no static version, dynamic declared, dist==__version__)."}, {"issue": "121", "title": "mypy is pinned to `python_version = \"3.11\"` but CI advertises and matrix-tests up to 3.14, so 3.12\u20133.14-only type errors are never caught", "file": "pyproject.toml", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "`[tool.mypy] python_version = \"3.11\"` forces mypy to type-check the code as if running on Python 3.11 regardless of the runner's interpreter. The core CI lane runs mypy across a 3.11\u20133.14 matrix and the integration lane runs it on 3.12, but because the analysis target is fixed at 3.11, type behavior that differs across versions (e.g. stdlib stubs that gained/changed signatures in 3.12+, version-gated typing constructs) is only ever validated at the 3.11 floor. The project classifies and matrix-t", "fix_update": "mypy python_version removed; NEW test test_mypy_is_not_pinned_to_a_single_python_version parses pyproject."}, {"issue": "122", "title": "run_study emits phase/reasoning spans but never starts tracing or stamps run_id baggage \u2014 traces cannot be correlated to the run or the experiment-tracker run_id", "file": "/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "run_study is the documented end-to-end composed runner and it opens trace spans for every phase (tracer.span('phase:INGEST') etc.) and the reasoning seam emits LLM/AGENT spans underneath. But run_study never calls bootstrap_tracing() and never wraps execution in run_trace_context(run_id) \u2014 grep of pipeline.py shows no reference to either. run_trace_context (tracing.py:145-163) is what stamps the 'assay.run_id' baggage onto every span so traces are correlatable across stores; the orchestration gr", "fix_update": "run_study calls bootstrap_tracing + wraps phases in run_trace_context(corr_id); NEW test test_run_study_correlates_spans_to_run_id asserts every phase span sees assay.run_id baggage + detached after."}]

content.text: Index 7 is issue #120 (package version hardcoded in two places). Let me read the relevant files.

content.content:
1	[build-system]
2	requires = ["hatchling"]
3	build-backend = "hatchling.build"
4	
5	[project]
6	name = "assay-engine"
7	dynamic = ["version"]  # single-sourced from src/assay_engine/__init__.py:__version__ (#120)
8	description = "Dataset-agnostic engine for rigorous, reproducible empirical ML/NLP on security & privacy data."
9	readme = "README.md"
10	requires-python = ">=3.11,<3.15"
11	license = "Apache-2.0"
12	license-files = ["LICENSE"]
13	authors = [{ name = "thehipsterciso" }]
14	keywords = ["empirical", "reproducibility", "methodology", "security", "privacy", "ml", "nlp"]
15	classifiers = [
16	    "Development Status :: 4 - Beta",
17	    "Intended Audience :: Science/Research",
18	    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
19	    "Operating System :: OS Independent",
20	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
21	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
22	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12",
23	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13",
24	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.14",
25	    "Topic :: Scientific/Engineering",
26	    "Topic :: Security",
27	    "Typing :: Typed",
28	]
29	# The engine core is dependency-free; heavy backends are optional extras (lazy-imported,
30	# ADR-0006). The composed runner and methodology work with no extras installed.
31	dependencies = []
32	
33	[project.urls]
34	Homepage = "https://github.com/thehipsterciso/hc-assay"
35	Repository = "https://github.com/thehipsterciso/hc-assay"
36	Issues = "https://github.com/thehipsterciso/hc-assay/issues"
37	
38	[project.optional-dependencies]
39	dev = [
40	    "pytest>=8",
41	    "pytest-cov>=5",  # CI integration lane runs `pytest --cov` (#103)
42	    "ruff>=0.6",
43	    "mypy>=1.11",
44	]
45	# Heavy backends for the reasoning seam — installed only where real LLM calls run.
46	# The engine imports and unit-tests (mocking the backends) without these.
47	reasoning = [
48	    "langchain-ollama>=1.1,<2",
49	    "claude-agent-sdk>=0.2.101,<0.3",
50	    "anyio>=4,<5",
51	]
52	observability = [
53	    "opentelemetry-sdk>=1.25,<2",
54	    "opentelemetry-exporter-otlp>=1.25,<2",
55	    "arize-phoenix>=7.0,<18",
56	    "openinference-instrumentation-langchain>=0.1.40,<1",
57	    "mlflow>=3.1,<4",
58	]
59	persistence = [
60	    "langgraph>=1.2,<2",  # core — provides MemorySaver (declared, not left transitive)
61	    "langgraph-checkpoint-postgres>=3.1,<4",
62	    "psycopg[binary,pool]>=3.2,<4",
63	    "qdrant-client>=1.9,<2",
64	]
65	orchestration = [
66	    "langgraph>=1.2,<2",
67	]
68	baseline = [
69	    "numpy>=1.26,<3",  # vectorized similarity at corpus scale (pure-Python fallback otherwise)
70	]
71	# Every backend in one shot (for a full local install). dev tooling stays separate.
72	all = [
73	    "assay-engine[reasoning,observability,persistence,orchestration,baseline]",
74	]
75	
76	[tool.hatch.version]
77	path = "src/assay_engine/__init__.py"
78	
79	[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
80	packages = ["src/assay_engine"]
81	
82	[tool.pytest.ini_options]
83	testpaths = ["tests"]
84	pythonpath = ["src"]
85	
86	[tool.ruff]
87	line-length = 100
88	src = ["src", "tests"]
89	
90	[tool.mypy]
91	# No fixed python_version: mypy infers the target from the running interpreter, so the CI
92	# matrix (3.11–3.14) type-checks against each version rather than only 3.11 (#121).
93	packages = ["assay_engine"]
94	strict = true
95	
96	# Optional reasoning/observability backends are not installed in the type-check env; their
97	# imports are lazy and guarded at runtime, so treat them as untyped third-party modules.
98	[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
99	module = [
100	    "langchain_ollama.*",
101	    "claude_agent_sdk.*",
102	    "anyio.*",
103	    "opentelemetry.*",
104	    "phoenix.*",
105	    "mlflow.*",
106	    "openinference.*",
107	    "langgraph.*",
108	    "qdrant_client.*",
109	    "psycopg.*",
110	    "psycopg_pool.*",
111	    "numpy.*",
112	]
113	ignore_missing_imports = true
114

1	"""assay_engine — the reusable, dataset-agnostic engine of the hc-assay blueprint.
2	
3	The single architectural rule (ADR-0002, ARCHITECTURE.md §3):
4	
5	    The engine never imports dataset specifics. Adapters implement the interfaces in
6	    ``assay_engine.contracts`` that the engine calls. A clone is: implement the adapter,
7	    register it, run.
8	
9	This package contains only goal-agnostic machinery:
10	
11	- ``contracts``      — the engine ↔ adapter boundary (canonical schema + adapter Protocols).
12	- ``methodology``    — hypotheses, the three verdicts, the two firewalls, the
13	                       measurement↔interpretation fence, confirmatory-test machinery.
14	- ``baseline``       — dataset-agnostic baseline builders (embeddings, similarity, graph,
15	                       clustering, descriptive stats).
16	- ``orchestration``  — the analysis graph, phase machine, and governance gates.
17	- ``reasoning``      — the tiered LLM reasoning seam.
18	- ``observability``  — self-hosted tracing + experiment tracking.
19	- ``persistence``    — durable checkpointing, data versioning, vector store.
20	- ``provenance``     — the append-only, hash-chained audit trail (GOVERNANCE §3).
21	- ``pipeline``       — ``run_study``: the composed, governed end-to-end runner (ADR-0010).
22	- ``registry``       — adapter registration.
23	
24	Nothing in this package may import an adapter module or name a specific dataset, authority,
25	or taxonomy.
26	"""
27	
28	__version__ = "0.0.1"
29	
30	# Public API — the surface a cloning study uses. Heavy/optional backends stay behind their
31	# subpackages (imported lazily there); these names are dependency-free to import.
32	from assay_engine.contracts import (  # noqa: E402
33	    ClaimRecord,
34	    Corpus,
35	    ExternalClaimsSource,
36	    FeatureBuilder,
37	    FeatureMatrix,
38	    IngestionParser,
39	    Relation,
40	    StudyDefinition,
41	    StudyMode,
42	    Unit,
43	)
44	from assay_engine.pipeline import (  # noqa: E402
45	    GateHandler,
46	    GateReview,
47	    IngestionError,
48	    StudyPlan,
49	    StudyResult,
50	    auto_approve,
51	    run_study,
52	)
53	from assay_engine.provenance import (  # noqa: E402
54	    ProvenanceEntry,
55	    ProvenanceError,
56	    ProvenanceTrail,
57	    from_records,
58	    verify_records,
59	)
60	from assay_engine.registry import (  # noqa: E402
61	    get_study,
62	    register_study,
63	    registered_studies,
64	)
65	
66	__all__ = [
67	    "__version__",
68	    # contracts
69	    "ClaimRecord",
70	    "Corpus",
71	    "ExternalClaimsSource",
72	    "FeatureBuilder",
73	    "FeatureMatrix",
74	    "IngestionParser",
75	    "Relation",
76	    "StudyDefinition",
77	    "StudyMode",
78	    "Unit",
79	    # pipeline
80	    "GateHandler",
81	    "GateReview",
82	    "IngestionError",
83	    "StudyPlan",
84	    "StudyResult",
85	    "auto_approve",
86	    "run_study",
87	    # provenance
88	    "ProvenanceEntry",
89	    "ProvenanceError",
90	    "ProvenanceTrail",
91	    "from_records",
92	    "verify_records",
93	    # registry
94	    "get_study",
95	    "register_study",
96	    "registered_studies",
97	]
98

content.text: The fix looks good: `version = "0.0.1"` is gone, replaced with `dynamic = ["version"]` and `[tool.hatch.version] path = "src/assay_engine/__init__.py"`. Now `__init__.py` is the single source of truth. Let me find and read the regression test.

content.content:
c4af6b4 fix(harden-1): address 2-agent confirmation gaps (10 fixes)
d0154e1 fix(harden-1): perf + graph-provenance batch (#106,#107,#111)
d9e5399 fix(harden-1): ops/supply-chain batch (#108,#120,#121)
6225498 fix(harden-1): provenance + observability batch (#109,#110,#113,#114,#119,#122)
5f4b3eb fix(harden-1): reliability + security batch (#104,#105,#112,#115,#116,#117,#118)
---
./tests/test_packaging.py

1	"""Packaging/config regression guards (pass-1 #120, #121)."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import tomllib
6	from pathlib import Path
7	
8	_PYPROJECT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "pyproject.toml"
9	
10	
11	def _pyproject() -> dict:
12	    return tomllib.loads(_PYPROJECT.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
13	
14	
15	def test_version_is_single_sourced_not_static():
16	    # #120: the version must be declared dynamic (read from __init__.__version__), with no second
17	    # static literal in pyproject that could drift from the module/provenance value.
18	    pp = _pyproject()
19	    assert "version" not in pp["project"], "static version literal reintroduced (drift risk)"
20	    assert "version" in pp["project"].get("dynamic", [])
21	    assert pp.get("tool", {}).get("hatch", {}).get("version", {}).get("path") == (
22	        "src/assay_engine/__init__.py"
23	    )
24	
25	
26	def test_version_matches_installed_distribution():
27	    import importlib.metadata as md
28	
29	    import assay_engine
30	
31	    try:
32	        dist = md.version("assay-engine")
33	    except md.PackageNotFoundError:  # pragma: no cover - only if not installed
34	        return
35	    assert dist == assay_engine.__version__
36	
37	
38	def test_mypy_is_not_pinned_to_a_single_python_version():
39	    # #121: a fixed python_version would hide 3.12-3.14-only type errors that the CI matrix
40	    # is supposed to catch.
41	    pp = _pyproject()
42	    assert "python_version" not in pp.get("tool", {}).get("mypy", {})
43

content.text: Now let me run the tests and verify the dynamic version actually resolves correctly via the build backend.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && .venv/bin/python -c "
import importlib.metadata as md, assay_engine
print('module __version__:', assay_engine.__version__)
try:
    print('dist version:', md.version('assay-engine'))
except Exception as e:
    print('dist not installed:', e)
"

content.content:
============================= test session starts ==============================
platform darwin -- Python 3.14.5, pytest-9.1.0, pluggy-1.6.0 -- /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python
cachedir: .pytest_cache

content.text:
Confirmed. There is now exactly one static version literal — `src/assay_engine/__init__.py:28`. The installed distribution metadata reads `Version: 0.0.1`, single-sourced via hatchling's dynamic version from that file. `determinism.py` imports `__version__` from the same file, so the provenance stamp and the package version can no longer drift.

Assessment of issue #120:

- **appropriate**: Yes. Root cause was two independent hardcoded version strings. The fix removes the static `version` from pyproject, declares `dynamic = ["version"]`, and points `[tool.hatch.version] path` at `__init__.py` — the file that already feeds the determinism/provenance manifest. This is the standard, sound single-source pattern, no hack or over-claim.

- **complete**: Yes. The finding is fully resolved (single source of truth) and has real regression tests. `test_version_is_single_sourced_not_static` asserts no static `version` key, that `dynamic` includes `version`, and that the hatch path is correct — I verified it actually fails (catches) when a static version is reintroduced. `test_version_matches_installed_distribution` asserts dist == `__version__`. All 3 tests pass; installed dist (0.0.1) matches module and metadata.

Relevant files:
- `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/pyproject.toml` (lines 7, 76-77)
- `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/__init__.py` (line 28)
- `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_packaging.py` (lines 15-35)

input.reasoning: Issue #120 (CONFIRMER #2, index 7). Root cause was two independent hardcoded version strings (pyproject `version=0.0.1` and `__init__.__version__`), with the latter feeding the determinism/provenance manifest, creating drift risk. APPROPRIATE: The fix removes the static `version` from pyproject (line 7 now `dynamic = ["version"]`) and adds `[tool.hatch.version] path = "src/assay_engine/__init__.py"` (lines 76-77), making the module attribute the single source of truth via hatchling — the standard, sound pattern, no hack. I confirmed exactly one static literal remains (`__init__.py:28`), and `determinism.py` imports `__version__` from that same file, so provenance stamp and package version can no longer diverge. COMPLETE: Finding fully resolved and backed by real regression tests. `test_version_is_single_sourced_not_static` asserts (a) no static `version` key in [project], (b) `version` in dynamic, (c) hatch.version.path correct — I verified it actually FAILS when a static version is reintroduced (catches the regression, not a pass-regardless test). `test_version_matches_installed_distribution` asserts dist == __version__. All 3 packaging tests pass; installed dist metadata reads Version: 0.0.1 == module __version__, proving hatchling resolves the dynamic version correctly. hatchling itself is build-time-only (not in venv) so I could not invoke the backend directly, but the installed dist metadata is conclusive evidence the dynamic path works.
input.evidence: pyproject.toml:7 `dynamic = ["version"]` (static `version` removed); pyproject.toml:76-77 `[tool.hatch.version] path = "src/assay_engine/__init__.py"`. Only static literal: src/assay_engine/__init__.py:28 `__version__ = "0.0.1"`. Installed dist metadata `Version: 0.0.1` == assay_engine.__version__ == 0.0.1 (md.version + distribution metadata). tests/test_packaging.py:15-35 (3 tests pass). Manually verified the single-sourced test catches a re-added static `version` (AssertionError raised). hatchling not installed in venv (build-time backend), so backend resolution verified indirectly via matching dist metadata.
